target: WIN-016 factual precision fixes: (1) supplementary cascade note 'six orders' → 'nine orders of magnitude'; (2) sections.json Gaia precision 'about a billionth' → 'about 3 billionths'

patches.description: WIN-016 supplementary cascade note: correct 'more than six orders of magnitude' to 'more than nine orders of magnitude'. Displacement from dome n(r) is ~multiple degrees (~5°); Gaia DR3 precision is ~10 microarcseconds = 2.78×10⁻⁹°. Ratio = ~5/2.78e-9 ≈ 1.8×10⁹ = nine orders of magnitude. The existing 'six orders of magnitude in frequency' elsewhere in the same entry is a different and correct claim (VLBI ~GHz vs Gaia ~500 THz) and must NOT be changed.
patches.find_pattern: incompatible with Gaia DR3 microarcsecond astrometry by more than six orders of magnitude.
patches.replace_pattern: incompatible with Gaia DR3 microarcsecond astrometry by more than nine orders of magnitude.

patches.description: sections.json part1.html: correct 'about a billionth of a degree' to 'about 3 billionths of a degree'. The text already states the correct numeric value (≈2.78 × 10⁻⁹°), which is 2.78 billionths, rounding to 3 billionths — not 1 billionth. The English gloss understates Gaia's precision by a factor of ~3.
patches.find_pattern: i.e. about a billionth of a degree
patches.replace_pattern: i.e. about 3 billionths of a degree

tldr_evidence: ISS-1754: WIN-016 supplementary cascade note compares dome n(r) stellar displacement ('multiple degrees', ~5°) to Gaia DR3 precision (10 µas = 2.78×10⁻⁹°). Ratio ≈ 1.8×10⁹ = nine orders of magnitude, not six. Note: the 'six orders of magnitude in frequency' in the main text (VLBI vs Gaia frequency span) is a separate, correct claim and is unchanged. ISS-1755: sections.json part1.html states Gaia precision as '≈2.78 × 10⁻⁹°, i.e. about a billionth of a degree'. 2.78×10⁻⁹ = 2.78 billionths ≈ 3 billionths, not 1 billionth. Fix is to align the English gloss with the numeric value already in the text.
tldr_verdict: No verdict changes. WIN-016 stays Refuted by Data. Both fixes are prose precision corrections that strengthen rather than soften the argument.
summary_for_decider: Two targeted string replacements. Patch 1: wins.json — one occurrence of 'more than six orders of magnitude' in the WIN-016 supplementary cascade note becomes 'more than nine orders of magnitude'. Patch 2: sections.json — 'about a billionth of a degree' becomes 'about 3 billionths of a degree'. Both changes are safe and reversible; neither touches verdict, category, or scoring.
